Home / UAE / Rental Yield Calculator

Dubai Rental Yield Calculator

Calculate your real ROI including all acquisition costs and expenses

Most "rental yield" numbers you see online show only gross yield. This calculator gives you the net yield — after DLD fee (4%), agent commission, service charges, vacancy, and maintenance. This is your actual return on investment.

Dubai Rental Yield Benchmarks

Below 3%Poor — consider other investments
3% - 5%Average — typical for prime areas
5% - 8%Good — above market average
8%+Excellent — verify the numbers

Your net yield of 3.6% is at the Dubai average of 5-7%.

Understanding Dubai Rental Yields

Dubai offers some of the highest rental yields globally (5-7% gross), primarily because:

  • No income tax: 100% of rental income is yours
  • No capital gains tax: Property appreciation is tax-free
  • High demand: Growing expatriate population (90%+ of residents are expats)
  • Transparent market: DLD publishes all transaction data

However, gross yield ≠ net yield. Acquisition costs (DLD 4% + agent 2%) reduce your first-year return significantly. Service charges in some buildings can be AED 15-25 per sqft, eating into rental income.

Rental Yields by Area (2026 Estimates)

AreaGross YieldTypical Price/sqft
JVC (Jumeirah Village Circle)7-9%AED 800-1,200
Discovery Gardens7-8%AED 500-700
Dubai Marina5-6%AED 1,500-2,500
Downtown Dubai4-5%AED 2,000-3,500
Palm Jumeirah3-4%AED 2,500-5,000+

Source: Dubai Land Department transaction data, Q1 2026 estimates. Actual yields vary by building, unit type, and furnishing.

Diversify beyond Dubai property

Property alone is concentrated risk. Sarwa gives you globally diversified ETFs from AED 500 — liquid, regulated.

Get started with Sarwa We may earn a commission if you sign up through our links — at no extra cost to you. This helps us keep every calculator on CalcStack free. Affiliate relationships never influence our rankings or calculator results.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is a good rental yield in Dubai?
Net yields of 5%+ are considered good. High-yield areas (JVC, Discovery Gardens, International City) can deliver 6-8% net. Premium areas (Downtown, Marina, Palm) typically yield 3-5% net but offer stronger capital appreciation potential.
How much is DLD fee when buying property?
4% of property value + AED 580 admin fee. This is the single largest transaction cost and must be factored into your yield calculation. Some developers offer to cover 50% as a promotional incentive.
Are rental yields in Dubai tax-free?
Yes. No income tax, no capital gains tax. There's a 5% municipality housing fee charged to tenants (not landlords) and a one-time 4% DLD fee at purchase.
